Records.description: One-loop massless box integral with three massless on-shell external legs and one off-shell leg $q=p_1+p_2+p_3$. Used as pedagogical example to demonstrate the differential equation method. The result is expressed in terms of Gauss $_2F_1$ hypergeometric functions for arbitrary space-time dimension $d$.

Records.authors: Herrmann, Enrico, Parra-Martinez, Julio, Ruf, Michael S., Zeng, Mao
Records.description: One-loop soft (linearized) box family for classical 2-to-2 massive scattering at $\mathcal{O}(G^2)$. Props 1-2 are linearized eikonal matter propagators for particles with 4-velocities $u_1,u_2$; props 3-4 are massless graviton propagators.

Records.authors: Dixon, Lance J., Drummond, James M., Henn, Johannes M.
Records.description: Scalar box integral in $D=6$ dimensions with two-mass-easy configuration. Result in terms of $\operatorname{Li}_2$ functions.
Four-loop massless on-shell box $G_4$, linearly reducible. Eps-expansion coefficients are HPLs of $s/u$.

1-loop traintrack: the four-mass box integral. 1-loop box with 4 massless propagators, 8 massless external legs grouped into 4 off-shell momenta $q_i$. Finite and dual-conformally invariant in $d=4$. Numerator ensures dual-conformal invariance. Evaluates to dilogarithms (weight 2). First evaluated by Hodges (1977). Depends on 1 dual-conformal cross-ratio.

Massless one-loop 4-point box integral family used as the main worked example for the intersection theory / dual form formalism. The paper computes the complete d-dimensional generalized unitarity coefficients for all one-loop 4-gluon helicity amplitudes (+++, -+++, --++, -+-+) by intersecting dual forms with cut integrands. A finite basis of 3 master integrals is used: 2 bubbles (s- and t-channel) and a finite 6-dimensional box (linear combination of the standard 4d box and bubbles). Results are given to all orders in epsilon and agree with known results of Bern et al.

Records.location: Section 2.1, code listing for Disteval usage example
Records.description: Massless one-loop box integral used as a pedagogical example to demonstrate the Disteval integration interface in pySecDec v1.6. Defined via LoopIntegralFromPropagators with 4 massless propagators and 4 massless external legs. Kinematics $p_1^2=p_2^2=p_3^2=0$ with Mandelstam variables $s=(p_1+p_2)^2$, $t=(p_2+p_3)^2$.
Divergent massless one-loop box $I_4^D(0,0,0,0;s_{12},s_{23};0,0,0,0)$. All internal and external lines massless. Contains double $1/\varepsilon^2$ IR poles. Result from Bern-Dixon-Kosower (1994).

One-loop massless box with 4 massless external legs. Used as the primary pedagogical example for the OPP integrand reduction method and van Neerven-Vermaseren parametrization. The integrand basis is derived (2 terms: $c_0 + c_1 l.\omega$) but the integral itself is not evaluated.

The massless one-loop four-point integral family used as a running pedagogical example throughout this review of intersection theory for Feynman integrals. The family contains four massless propagators depending on Mandelstam invariants $s$ and $t$, with all external legs massless. The paper identifies three master integrals (box, triangle, bubble) via Euler characteristic computation and uses them to illustrate the gauge-theoretic structure of differential equations on the kinematic space, basis decomposition via intersection numbers, and the two perturbative limits of intersection pairings.

Records.authors: Caola, Fabrizio, Chakraborty, Amlan, Gambuti, Giulio, von Manteuffel, Andreas, Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: One-loop planar integral family for massless four-quark scattering $q\bar{q} \to \bar{Q} Q$ in QCD. Standard massless box topology with 4 propagators. All external momenta taken incoming with $p_1+p_2+p_3+p_4=0$. Results expressed in harmonic polylogarithms of $x=-t/s$.
One-loop massless box computed via Feynman-parameter-space IBP identities and DEs. Solved to $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon)$ recovering known result. Methodological example for parametric IBP.

One-loop massless box with four massless external legs and four massless internal propagators. The Baikov polynomial is given explicitly as a quadratic polynomial in $z_1,\ldots,z_4$. The diagonal basis has 3 master integrals. Used as a benchmark for the massless case where the diagonal basis prescription works without modification.

One-loop massless 2->2 box integral family with all external legs on-shell, used as the running pedagogical example for IBP reduction and the canonical-form differential-equation method. Three master integrals are chosen (s- and t-channel bubbles plus the scalar box, basis $g_1,g_2,g_3$ in Eq. (3.27)). The dimensionless variable is $x=t/s$. The DE in canonical form has alphabet $\{x,1+x\}$ with singular points $x=0,-1,\infty$ (corresponding to $t=0, u=0, s=0$). Boundary fixed by requiring regularity of the planar box at $x=-1$. Result for $g_3$ given through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^3)$ in classical polylogarithms ($\operatorname{Li}_2, \operatorname{Li}_3$) in Eq. (3.32).
Records.familyName: one-loop massless box (2->2) family / one-loop massless box

Records.description: One-loop massless box with four massless external legs; used to demonstrate degenerate multivariate residue computation for integrals with doubled propagators via Groebner basis transformation law

Records.authors: Barakat, Mohamed, Brüser, Robin, Fieker, Claus, Huber, Tobias, Piclum, Jan
Records.description: Massless one-loop box with four massless on-shell external legs. $V_4$ (Klein four-group) symmetry. Three master integrals: $I(1,1,1,1), I(1,1,0,1), I(1,1,1,0)$. Normal-form IBP relations computed via both GB and LA ansatz.

One-loop massless on-shell box used as the introductory FIESTA4 example. Numerical evaluation via SDEvaluate[UF[...]] with all unit indices through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$. Numerical kinematics $S=3, T=1$ are substituted before integration. Result quoted in the paper: $-4.38658 + 1.3333/\varepsilon^2 - 0.732466/\varepsilon + \mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$. Same diagram is also re-used in the SDExpand and SDAnalyze examples (showing poles at $d=2$ and $d=4$).

Massless on-shell one-loop scalar box with double IR pole $4/(\varepsilon^2 s t)$. Standard closed form through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$ with $\ln$, $\ln^2$ and $-8 \zeta_2$ contributions. Reference compendium expression for the 0-mass box used as IR-divergent building block.
Records.location: Appendix 'Scalar one-loop integrals', four-point functions, line ~152

Records.description: One-loop scalar box $D^{(1)}(k_1^2,k_2^2,k_3^2,k_4^2,s,t)$ with massless internal propagators and all four external momenta off-shell. Via a two-fold MB representation, reduces to the one-loop triangle via variable pairing $X=k_1^2 k_3^2/(s t), Y=k_2^2 k_4^2/(s t)$, giving $D^{(1)} = (i \pi^2/(s t)) \Phi^{(1)}(X,Y)$.
Records.familyName: one-loop massless box with four off-shell external legs

Records.description: Massless one-loop box, Section 5.1 warm-up for the epsilon-factorisation algorithm via twisted cohomology and loop-by-loop Baikov.
One-loop massless box, warm-up. Three MIs reduced via IBP; canonical DE built; analytic solution through $\varepsilon^1$. Used to introduce the neural-network DE-solving pipeline.
Massless one-loop box example for top-down recursive Baikov reduction without computing intersection numbers. Three masters (box + two bubble channels).
1-loop 4-point dlog integrand family; 5 dlog integrands (1 box + 4 triangles) via DlogBasis.

Scalar one-loop box integral $D_0(s_1,s_2,q^2)$ with four massless internal propagators and one off-shell external leg ($p_1^2=p_2^2=p_3^2=0, q^2=(p_1+p_2+p_3)^2 \neq 0$). Computed in $d=4-2\varepsilon$ via Feynman parametrization with a factorization that decouples into 1D integrals, giving an all-order epsilon expansion in single-valued polylogarithms. The $+i0$ prescription is kept explicit so real and imaginary parts are determined in all kinematic regions. Appendix A handles vanishing Mandelstam limits.

One-loop massless four-point (zero-mass box) integral in the parametric representation with Symanzik polynomials $U = \sum \alpha_i$ and $F = \alpha_1 \alpha_3 s + \alpha_2 \alpha_4 t$. The paper constructs the annihilating ideal via twisted Griffiths-Dwork reduction, finds D-module holonomic rank $r=3$ with singular locus $s t (s+t)$, and derives a third-order Picard-Fuchs operator factorizing as a product of three first-order operators in $d-s$. No explicit master integral evaluation is performed; result is the set of annihilators and the PF equation.
Records.location: Section Applications a), Eqs. (around eq:massless_box_integrand, eq:ann_massless_box, eq:pf_massless_box)

Records.description: Scalar one-loop four-point box with all external and internal lines massless. The answer is written as a generalized Fox H function exact in epsilon ($d=4+\varepsilon$).

One-loop massless on-shell box used as the worked example for noncommutative rational double-shift Groebner basis IBP reduction. 9-element reduced Groebner basis (Eq. 4.2); 3 masters $\{J(1,1,1,1), J(1,1,0,1), J(1,1,1,0)\}$ — box and two triangles (Eq. 4.4). Normal forms of $D_i$ and $a_i D_i^-$ given in Eqs. (4.5)-(4.6); FORM implementation reduces $J(10,10,10,10)$ in under 10 seconds with parametric propagator powers.

One-loop massless box with four on-shell massless external legs and Mandelstams $s,t$. Parametric representation regularized, expanded in eps, converted to fibration basis in $[-s/-t, s/-t]$; reproduces the classic $1/\varepsilon^2 + 1/\varepsilon +$ finite result.
One-loop massless box with 4 propagators, 3 canonical MIs (two bubbles + box). Used to demonstrate Integrated Unitarity method. Called 'one-loop box' in the paper.

One-loop massless box integral for 2-to-2 scattering in $d=6-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. Canonical DE basis with 3 master integrals including bubbles with raised propagator powers (baubles). Solved in HPLs with indices $\{0,-1\}$.
Records.familyName: massless 1-loop 4-point box in six dimensions (4 propagators)

Records.description: One-loop massless box topology used as the primary example for developing diagonal, matrix-diagonal, and triangular IBP diagonalization algorithms. All four external legs are massless. 3 master integrals identified.

One-loop scalar box integral with four massless propagators and two non-adjacent off-shell external legs ($p_1^2=p_3^2=0, p_2^2$ and $p_4^2$ nonzero). Closed-form result as four Gauss $_2F_1$ functions, and equivalently in single-valued polylogarithms allowing all-order epsilon expansion with explicit real and imaginary parts.
Massless one-loop box used to demonstrate parameter-space IBP and DEs. 4x4 system solved via Magnus exponentiation through $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon)$.
One-loop massless box used to illustrate higher residue pairing method. Connection in epsilon-form. 3 MIs. Alphabet $\{s,t,s+t\}$.

One-loop massless box integral. Canonical Pfaffian system derived via Macaulay matrix method. GKZ rank 3 with standard monomials $\{d^2, d, 1\}$. Canonical basis given in eq. (5.32). Also used in Section 7 to demonstrate integral decomposition via cohomology intersection numbers. Decomposition of $I(4,2,1,1,1)$ verified against LiteRed.

Records.authors: Frellesvig, Hjalte, Gasparotto, Federico, Laporta, Stefano, Mandal, Manoj K., Mastrolia, Pierpaolo, Mattiazzi, Luca et al.
Records.description: One-loop massless box demonstrating multivariate intersection number decomposition. Reduced to 3 master integrals.

1-loop traintrack: the four-mass box integral. 1-loop box with 4 massless propagators, 8 massless external legs grouped into 4 off-shell momenta q_i. Finite and dual-conformally invariant in d=4. Numerator ensures dual-conformal invariance. Evaluates to dilogarithms (weight 2). First evaluated by Hodges (1977). Depends on 1 dual-conformal cross-ratio.

Massless one-loop 4-point box integral family used as the main worked example for the intersection theory / dual form formalism. The paper computes the complete d-dimensional generalized unitarity coefficients for all one-loop 4-gluon helicity amplitudes (+++, -+++, --++, -+-+) by intersecting dual forms with cut integrands. A finite basis of 3 master integrals is used: 2 bubbles (s- and t-channel) and a finite 6-dimensional box (linear combination of the standard 4d box and bubbles). Results are given to all orders in epsilon and agree with known results of Bern et al.
Records.location: eqs. (eq:D1)-(eq:D4), Section 5.1 (Conventions for massless 4-particle kinematics); UT basis in Section 2.3; amplitude results in Section 6.1

Records.description: One-loop massless box with 4 massless external legs. Used as the primary pedagogical example for the OPP integrand reduction method and van Neerven-Vermaseren parametrization. The integrand basis is derived (2 terms: c_0 + c_1 l.omega) but the integral itself is not evaluated.

Massless one-loop box integral used as a pedagogical example to demonstrate the Disteval integration interface in pySecDec v1.6. Defined via LoopIntegralFromPropagators with 4 massless propagators and 4 massless external legs. Kinematics p1^2=p2^2=p3^2=0 with Mandelstam variables s=(p1+p2)^2, t=(p2+p3)^2.
Records.location: Section 2.1, code listing for Disteval usage example

Records.authors: Bern, Zvi, Enciso, Michael, Ita, Harald, Zeng, Mao
Records.description: One-loop massless box, used to demonstrate DE generation from dual conformal transformations. Reproduces canonical DE form. No integral evaluation.

Records.authors: Gambuti, Giulio, Kosower, David A., Novichkov, Pavel P., Tancredi, Lorenzo
Records.description: Massless one-loop box used as the pedagogical example for the finite-numerator algorithm. The Landau analysis identifies four collinear and four soft IR-divergent surfaces. Within a 24-dimensional UV-finite ansatz at rank three, 12 directions are locally finite. The ideal of locally finite numerators has three rank-two generators expressible as products of Gram determinants.

Massless one-loop scalar box integral I(s,t) in d=4-2*eps. Computed exactly (non-perturbatively in eps) in closed form as a sum of two 2F1 hypergeometric functions of arguments 1+s/t and 1+t/s (Eq. 2.7), with Laurent expansion through O(eps^2) reproducing the standard 1/eps^2 + Li_2 result of Eq. (2.9). Also derived via the Mellin-Barnes representation in Section 3 (Eq. 3.4), shown to agree with the direct method after analytic continuation of the hypergeometric functions; spurious terms cancel in Eq. (3.18).
Records.location: Section 2, Eq. (2.1); Mellin-Barnes form Section 3, Eq. (3.4)

Names: one-loop massless box with four off-shell external legs
massless 1-loop 4-point box in six dimensions (4 propagators)

-\frac{2}{\varepsilon^2 \left(s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)\right)}+\frac{2 \left(\log \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)+\log \left(s_{12} M^{-2}\right)\right)}{\varepsilon s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)}-\frac{12 H\left(\frac{M^2}{M^2-s_{12}},\frac{s_{12}+s_{23}}{s_{23}}\,;1\right)-12 H\left(0,\frac{s_{12}+s_{23}}{s_{23}}\,;1\right)+24 H(-1,-1\,;1)+6 \log ^2\left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)+12 \log \left(\frac{s_{12}}{s_{12}+s_{23}}\right) \log \left(-\frac{-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}}{s_{12}}\right)+12 \log \left(s_{12} M^{-2}\right) \log \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)-12 \log \left(s_{12} M^{-2}\right) \log \left(-\frac{-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}}{s_{12}}\right)-6 \log ^2\left(s_{12} M^{-2}\right)-12 \zeta(2)+6 \log ^2\left(\frac{s_{12}}{s_{12}+s_{23}}\right)+\pi ^2-12 \log ^2(2)}{6 \left(s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)\right)}+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
Results.normalizedSymbolTeX: \begin{aligned}\varepsilon^{-2}&\colon\;-\frac{2}{s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)} \\ \varepsilon^{-1}&\colon\;-\frac{2}{s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)}\!\left[-(W_1) - (W_2)\right] \\ \varepsilon^{0}&\colon\;-\frac{2}{s_{12} \left(-M^2+s_{12}+s_{23}\right)}\!\left[(W_2 \otimes W_1) + (W_2 \otimes W_3) - (W_4 \otimes W_1) + (W_1 \otimes W_1) + (W_1 \otimes W_2) + (W_2 \otimes W_2) - (W_3 \otimes W_2) - (W_2 \otimes W_3)\right]\end{aligned}
